title: Build Reliable AI Tutorials
description: A compact sample specification for an end-to-end tutorial on creating reliable AI-generated lessons.

learningObjectives.description: Classify generated claims as verified, verify, or unsupported based on available evidence.
Map each claim to its supporting citation or to a source-plan task.
Decide whether to publish, mark for verification, or remove each claim.

lessons.title: Classify Claims and Map Evidence
lessons.summary: Classify generated claims, map each to evidence, and decide whether to publish, verify, or remove.

concepts.definition: A deterministic check that generated content must pass before publication.
A label indicating whether a factual statement is verified, needs verification, or is unsupported.

misconceptions.misconception: A model-generated lesson is ready when it sounds polished.
misconceptions.correction: Generated content still needs schema validation, source checks, accessibility checks, and human review.

workedExamples.title: Classify a popularity claim
workedExamples.prompt: A generated lesson says 'Library X is the recommended option for new projects' but the source plan has no citation.
steps: Check the source plan — no citation is listed for this claim.
The claim is plausible but 'recommended' implies consensus that may not exist.
Add a source-plan entry to find a recent, authoritative comparison.
Wrap the claim in a VerifyClaim component with status='verify'.
workedExamples.explanation: The verify state keeps plausible claims visible for review rather than silently publishing or removing them.

exercises.prompt: Classify three claims (WCAG requirement with source, screen-reader statistic without source, browser rendering guarantee without source) and decide: publish, verify, or remove.

answers.answer: Claim A (WCAG requirement): verified — publish with citation. Claim B (screen-reader statistic): verify — add source-plan task. Claim C (identical rendering): unsupported — remove.
rubric: Correctly identifies all three claim states
Justifies each decision with evidence reasoning

formativeAssessment.prompt: A generated lesson states a tool is the most popular but cites no source. What is the correct classification?
formativeAssessment.expectedAnswer: verify — the claim is plausible but lacks a cited source. Add a source-plan task.

formativeAssessment.prompt: After classifying a claim as verify, what two actions should you take?
formativeAssessment.expectedAnswer: Add a citation task to the source plan and wrap the claim in a VerifyClaim component in the MDX.

formativeAssessment.prompt: When should you remove a claim rather than marking it for verification?
formativeAssessment.expectedAnswer: When no credible source is likely to exist or the claim contradicts established facts.

masteryCriteria.description: Can classify claims as verified, verify, or unsupported based on evidence availability.
masteryCriteria.threshold: Correctly classifies four out of five sample claims.

masteryCriteria.description: Can map each classified claim to a citation or source-plan task.
masteryCriteria.threshold: Produces a complete evidence map for a set of three claims.

masteryCriteria.description: Can justify publish, verify, or remove decisions for each claim.
masteryCriteria.threshold: Correctly decides and justifies action for four out of five claims.

recap: Use structured specs to constrain generation.
Treat citations and verification states as required content, not polish.
nextSteps: Author a source plan for a real tutorial.
Run pnpm validate before requesting review.

culturalAssumptions: Examples assume familiarity with command-line development.
